ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002809 covers nasi lemak as a dish category; this specific set variant with chicken sausage, cuttlefish, and chicken meat loaf is not individually coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP represents nasi lemak as a dish; the specific set composition with sausage, cuttlefish, and meat loaf is not individually enumerated. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for nasi lemak or this specific mixed rice set meal.

allergens: gluten (chicken sausage, chicken meat loaf may contain wheat)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard nasi lemak set plate comprising approximately 200 g coconut rice, 80 g mixed protein accompaniments (chicken sausage, cuttlefish fillet, chicken meat loaf), one hard-boiled egg (~50 g), sambal chilli, ikan bilis, and peanuts (~120 g combined), totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Lemak (with egg, ikan bilis, peanuts, and sambal) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo provides nutrient data for standard nasi lemak with egg, ikan bilis, peanuts, and sambal. This set adds chicken sausage, cuttlefish fillet, and chicken meat loaf. Per-100g values are estimated by weighted-component adaptation using HPB nasi lemak base values and HPB/USDA SR Legacy values for chicken sausage (~15 g per 100 g set), cuttlefish (~18 g per 100 g set), and chicken meat loaf (~17 g per 100 g set). The adaptation increases protein, fat, and sodium relative to the plain nasi lemak base. Values represent a best-estimate composite per 100 g of the full set.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white rice and coconut rice analogues; Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific nasi lemak set. GI of 55 is estimated using the University of Sydney GI Database entry for coconut milk rice / white rice with accompaniments (GI range 50–60 for mixed rice meals with protein and fat). The protein and fat content from sausage, cuttlefish, and meat loaf moderates the glycemic response relative to plain white rice (GI ~72). A GI of 55 is classified as 'low' per standard GI definitions (<=55 low GI) and is the defensible analogous estimate per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 93.6) / 100 ≈ 51.5, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 20.8 × 4.5 = 93.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1890 mg per set serving) from sambal, ikan bilis, chicken sausage, and chicken meat loaf
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this meal to occasional consumption and request reduced sambal and ikan bilis to lower sodium intake, as a single serving may provide up to 80–95% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) but high glycemic load per serving (~51.5 g) due to the large portion of coconut rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced rice portion (100–120 g cooked rice) and pair it with the protein accompaniments to blunt postprandial glucose excursions, as the full set serving delivers a high glycemic load that may significantly elevate blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.1 g per 100 g) from coconut milk rice and processed meat components, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal due to its combined saturated fat and sodium burden; choosing a smaller rice portion, removing the sausage, and requesting less sambal can meaningfully reduce cardiovascular risk per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Lemak Set 1 from Qi Ji (Funan, Singapore) is a complete Singaporean rice set meal centred on fragrant pandan-infused coconut rice, a dish considered a national comfort food of Singapore and Malaysia. The coconut rice is cooked by simmering long-grain rice in coconut milk with pandan leaves, yielding a rich, slightly sweet, and aromatic base with a soft, slightly sticky texture. The set is served with a signature sambal chilli — a cooked paste of dried chillies, shrimp paste (belacan), onions, and tamarind — which provides a spicy, savoury, and slightly tangy flavour profile. Accompaniments include crispy deep-fried ikan bilis (dried anchovies), roasted peanuts, and a hard-boiled egg. This Set 1 variant adds three protein items: a chicken meat loaf (luncheon-style processed chicken product, mild and savoury), a cuttlefish fillet (chewy, mildly briny, often marinated in sambal or soy-based sauce), and a chicken sausage (processed, mildly spiced). The full set is a nutritionally dense meal providing approximately 878 kcal per 450 g serving, with meaningful protein (~38 g), moderate-to-high fat (~41 g, predominantly from coconut milk and processed meats), and a substantial carbohydrate load (~94 g) from the rice. Sodium is notably high (~1890 mg per serving) due to the processed meat components and sambal. Clinically, this meal is of concern for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and glycemic load. It is halal-certified in most Singaporean hawker and restaurant contexts. The dish is widely available at hawker centres, food courts, and casual dining chains across Singapore.

Nasi lemak with sausage cuttlefish calories

confidence_reason: HPB Singapore Foodo provides a validated nutrient profile for standard nasi lemak with egg, ikan bilis, peanuts, and sambal. This set adds three protein components (chicken sausage, cuttlefish fillet, chicken meat loaf) not individually measured in the HPB entry. Per-100g values are adapted by weighted-component estimation using HPB and USDA SR Legacy reference values for each additional protein item. Confidence is medium because no single approved source covers this exact set composition; the adaptation is methodologically sound but introduces estimation uncertainty, particularly for sodium and fat from the processed meat components.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo nasi lemak entry or the USDA SR Legacy references used for the protein components. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary or secondary sources used for this composite estimate. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in HPB Foodo or the USDA SR Legacy entries used for adaptation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc are estimated from HPB and USDA SR Legacy component data and carry moderate uncertainty. The serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Singaporean nasi lemak set portions; actual restaurant portion may vary. Halal status is assumed based on Qi Ji's general halal certification in Singapore; consumers should verify current certification. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value of 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Mixed' to accurately reflect the composite meal nature. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific set exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
